chunk_summary: This section outlines dietary assessment and feeding skills evaluation workflows for outpatient and inpatient settings in India, emphasizing task-shifting, structured tools, and integration with national programs like ICDS/POSHAN for managing malnutrition and feeding challenges.
learning_objectives: Understand the tiered approach for dietary assessment in outpatient settings, including screening and structured recall.
Learn inpatient feeding assessment protocols, including daily monitoring and discharge feed observation.
Recognize the role of national programs like ICDS/POSHAN in supporting malnourished children.
Identify escalation triggers for advanced feeding interventions in severe cases.
key_facts: Outpatient dietary assessment includes a 2–3 minute screen for all children, followed by detailed evaluation for high-risk cases.
Inpatient monitoring involves daily intake charts tracking offered versus consumed food, vomiting, and other symptoms.
Discharge feed observation is critical for infants, children with CP, and those with recurrent respiratory symptoms.
Nutrition Rehabilitation Centers (NRCs) in India focus on transitioning children with SAM from therapeutic feeds to home diets.
Appetite testing and daily monitoring guide decisions on supervised oral feeds versus escalation to advanced care.
Linking families to ICDS/POSHAN and promoting calorie-dense local foods are key to sustainable recovery.
reference_quote: WHO: identification of severe acute malnutrition requiring inpatient care in children 6–59 months — https://www.who.int/tools/elena/interventions/sam-...
document_key_topics: Feeding skills evaluation in inpatient care
Integration of national nutrition programs in India
